description: Philippine national health insurance claims capability via PhilHealth eClaims. Covers electronic claims submission (CF1-CF4 format), eligibility verification, and remittance processing for PhilHealth, the universal health coverage program. Requires regulatory compliance pack, national coding standards pack, locale pack, and the PhilHealth eClaims connector adapter.

readiness.evidenceSummary: Payer pack manifest (payer-philhealth.json) identifies PhilHealth as the target payer with government-portal integration mode. Configuration keys for claim format and payer identifier are declared. The required adapter (philhealth-eclaims-connector) is not implemented — fallback is integration-pending. The archive repo contains a prototype PhilHealth connector (Phase 38/40) that is reference material only and not production code.
readiness.scopeBounds: PhilHealth only (1 of 15+ PH payers). Private HMO payer integrations are not covered. Single-payer capability bound.
constrainingFactors: payer-philhealth pack is draft — no connector configuration or rules exist.
philhealth-eclaims-connector adapter is not implemented. Current status: integration-pending.
Depends on regulatory-philhealth-doh (draft) and standards-ph (draft) which are also blocking.
Requires rcm-module license entitlement (declared in pack eligibility).
Archive repo prototype is reference only — not ported to platform repo.
No claim submission, eligibility, or remittance processing has been tested.

packDependencies.rationale: PhilHealth claims capability is directly provided by the PhilHealth payer pack.
PhilHealth claims require DOH regulatory compliance context. Claim submission rules derive from the regulatory framework.
PhilHealth claims require ICD-10-CM and RVS coding per national standards. Claim validation depends on correct coding.
PhilHealth claims require PH locale conventions for currency (PHP), date formatting, and payer-specific field formatting.

claimPosture.permittedScopeStatement: PhilHealth has been identified as the target national payer for the PH market in a planning-stage pack manifest. No claims submission, eligibility verification, or remittance processing capability exists. The connector adapter is not implemented.
prohibitedClaims: Must not claim 'PhilHealth billing support' or 'PhilHealth integration'.
Must not claim claims submission capability for any PH payer.
Must not claim eligibility verification for PhilHealth members.
Must not reference archive repo prototype as current platform capability.
Must not claim PH billing readiness based on this capability alone.

visibilityPosture.rationale: PhilHealth billing is visible in control-plane for PH market planning but blocked by multiple unmet dependencies. Hidden from tenant admins until adapter is implemented and packs reach published state.

activationConstraints: payer-philhealth pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
regulatory-philhealth-doh pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
standards-ph pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
locale-ph pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
philhealth-eclaims-connector adapter must be implemented and at least in sandbox mode.
Tenant must have rcm-module license entitlement.

notes: This is the most dependency-heavy capability in the current PH seed corpus.
PhilHealth uses CF1-CF4 JSON bundles, NOT X12 EDI format (see archive repo Phase 40 note).
Archive repo (Phase 38/40) contains a prototype connector and serializer — reference material only.
PhilHealth covers 1 of 15+ PH payers. Private HMO payers (Maxicare, Intellicare, etc.) require separate payer packs.
This is a gating blocker for PH market payer-readiness dimension.
Single-payer bound: claim posture cannot be widened to multi-payer without additional payer pack instances.
